message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To understand the effect of COVID-19 lockdown measures on severity of illness and mortality in non-COVID-19 acute medical admissions.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A prospective observational study</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Setting</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    3 large acute medical receiving units in NHS Lothian, Scotland. Participants: Non-covid-19 acute admissions (n = 1756) were examined over the first 31 days after the implementation of the COVID-19 lockdown policy in the United Kingdom on 23
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    March 2019. Patients admitted over a matched interval in the previous 5 years were used as a comparator cohort (n = 14961).
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcome measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Patient demography, biochemical markers of clinical acuity and 7-day hospital inpatient mortality.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Non-covid-19 acute medical admissions reduced by a mean 43.8% (95% CI 27.3, 59.4) across all 3 sites in comparison to the mean of the preceding 5 years P < 0.001. The reduction in admissions predominated in the over 75 age category and a greater proportion arrived by emergency ambulance transport. Non-covid-19 admissions during lockdown had a greater incidence of severe renal injury, hyperlactataemia and over twice the risk of hospital death within 7 days 5.01% vs 2.49% which persisted after adjustment for confounders (OR 2.17, 95% CI 1.70,2.73, P < 0.0001)</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>These data support current fears that patients are delaying seeking medical attention for acute illness which is associated with worsening clinical parameters and a higher risk of death following admission.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Higher clinical acuity and 7-day hospital mortality in non-COVID-19 acute medical admissions: prospective observational study

reference.unstructured: UK Government. Staying at home and away from others (social distancing). https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/full-guidance-on-staying-at-home-and-away-from-others
